explanation: The property states that globally, if the system is in the self-test mode (bool_exp1), then eventually the test for the functionality of the expiratory valve will pass (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that the self-test procedure shall confirm the expiratory valve is functional, implying that if the self-test is active, its success condition for the expiratory valve must eventually be met.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'SelfTestMode' (the self-test procedure is active), it must eventually lead to 'testPSExpPass' (the expiratory valve functionality being confirmed). This captures the requirement that the self-test procedure will successfully confirm the valve's functionality.

The property ensures that whenever the overall self-test procedure successfully passes, the test specifically for the functionality of the expiratory valve also indicates success. This captures the requirement that the self-test procedure must confirm the proper functioning of the expiratory valve.

The property states that globally (always), if the system is in 'SelfTestMode', it must eventually be the case that the 'testPSExpPass' (test for functionality of the expiratory valve passes) becomes true. This directly captures the requirement that the self-test procedure shall confirm the expiratory valve is functional, implying a future success of the test while in the self-test mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is in SelfTestMode, then the test for the functionality of the expiratory valve must pass. This captures the requirement that the self-test procedure shall confirm the expiratory valve is functional throughout its execution.

The property states that whenever the system is in SelfTestMode, it must eventually confirm that the expiratory valve is functional. This captures the requirement that the self-test procedure 'shall confirm' the functionality of the expiratory valve, meaning that its successful test is an eventual outcome when the system is in this mode.

The property states that globally, it is always the case that if all self-test steps have passed successfully (selfTestPass), then it must be true that the test for the functionality of the expiratory valve also passed successfully (testPSExpPass). This captures the requirement that a successful overall self-test procedure confirms the functionality of the expiratory valve.

The property states that whenever the system is in 'SelfTestMode' (the self-test procedure is active), it must eventually lead to the 'testPSExpPass' (the test for the expiratory valve's functionality passes). This captures the requirement that the self-test procedure 'shall confirm' the expiratory valve is functional.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'SelfTestMode', it must eventually be the case that 'testPSExpPass' (the test for the expiratory valve functionality passes) within a specified duration. This captures the requirement that the self-test procedure shall confirm the expiratory valve is functional, implying a successful outcome of the test within a reasonable timeframe.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'SelfTestMode', it must be confirmed that the expiratory valve passes its functionality test ('testPSExpPass'). This directly translates 'The self-test procedure shall confirm the expiratory valve is functional'.
